intro: Lebbaeus, surnamed Thaddaeus, was one of the twelve apostles (Matt. 10:3), identified with Judas (not Iscariot) in Luke 6:16 and Acts 1:13 — sometimes designated "Judas the son of James." The name Lebbaeus is variously interpreted as related to the Hebrew word for "heart" (leb) or as meaning "courageous." He posed the only question attributed to him in the Gospels at the Last Supper, asking why Jesus would reveal himself to the disciples but not to the world (John 14:22). Ancient tradition assigns him missionary activity in Persia and Armenia.
